description: Sector overlay for South African exploration, mining, mineral rights, mine health and safety, environmental authorization, and community/social-labour documentation.

metadata.scope: South Africa-only sector overlay controls for Mining. Intended to be used with south_africa_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official South African statutes, regulators, guidance, and sector sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: Mineral and Petroleum Resources Development Act, 2002
primary_sources.issuer: Department of Mineral and Petroleum Resources

primary_sources.name: Mine Health and Safety Act, 1996 and Mine Health and Safety Inspectorate
primary_sources.issuer: DMPR / Mine Health and Safety Inspectorate

primary_sources.name: Mine Health and Safety Inspectorate resource centre

rules.title: Mining or prospecting right evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for mining right, prospecting right, permit, DMPR approval, or mineral title evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Mine health and safety evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for Mine Health and Safety Act, health and safety plan, inspection, audit, or incident-control evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: Mine Health and Safety Act / MHSI

rules.title: Mandatory code of practice evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for mandatory COP, code of practice, hazard-specific procedures, or MHSI guideline evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Environmental authorization or rehabilitation evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for environmental authorization, EMP/EMPr, rehabilitation, closure, waste, or water-use controls.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Social and labour plan or community obligation evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for social and labour plan, community development, local procurement, or labour obligation evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
